rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical anti-Markovnikov dihydroxylation of alkenes to 1,2-diols via iodonium intermediates and α-amino radical-mediated C-I activation in tBuOH/H2O
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of iodide to I2 (which acts as the alkene-activating mediator forming an iodonium) and parallel anodic oxidation of in-situ-generated Et3N (from Et4NI decomposition) to an α-amino radical; cathode reduces protons to H2

key_intermediates.name: β-iodo alcohol from water opening of iodonium (Int-II)
halogen-bonded / HAT transition state A between Int-II and α-amino radical
free epoxide (Int-IV) in equilibrium with Int-III

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes I- to I• which dimerizes to I2; I2 forms an iodonium with the alkene that is opened by water to give a β-iodo alcohol (Int-II). In parallel, Et4NI decomposes to Et3N which is anodically oxidized (-H+/-e-) to an α-amino radical that abstracts the iodine of Int-II; the resulting carbon radical loses an electron at the anode and cyclizes to a protonated epoxide (Int-III), which is opened by H2O to deliver the trans 1,2-diol (with Int-IV epoxide as equilibrium partner). Protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.
rate_determining_step: Iodine-atom abstraction from Int-II by the α-amino radical (C-I activation) followed by intramolecular cyclization to Int-III

probe_reagents.name: compound 4 (β-iodo alcohol) without electricity, with Et3N
probe_reagents.observation: No product 2a; 4 recovered in 90% — electricity required to convert Int-II to product

probe_reagents.name: compound 4 with electricity + Et3N
probe_reagents.observation: 2a formed in 27%, confirming Int-II is on-cycle and electrochemistry drives its conversion

probe_reagents.name: compound 4 with electricity + DIPEA (in place of Et3N)
probe_reagents.observation: 2a in 11%; tertiary amine + current still operative but less efficient

probe_reagents.name: GC-MS of reaction mixture
probe_reagents.observation: Et3N detected (m/z 86, 58, 101), confirming in-situ decomposition of Et4NI to Et3N

probe_reagents.name: single-crystal X-ray of product 2y (CCDC-2286663)
probe_reagents.observation: trans-diol configuration on cyclohexane confirms anti opening of a protonated epoxide (Int-III/Int-IV) by water

notes: An earlier mechanism draft proposed direct Int-II → Int-III without electron accounting; reviewer 3 pointed out the missing electron loss, and the revised mechanism explicitly invokes anodic oxidation of the carbon radical (after iodine atom transfer to the α-amino radical) to give the cyclic oxiranium Int-III. Int-III and the neutral epoxide Int-IV are in protonation equilibrium; trans-diol stereochemistry from internal alkenes (X-ray of 2y) supports SN2-like anti opening of the protonated epoxide by water. Et4NI serves dual roles: iodide source for I2 mediator and precursor (via Hofmann-type decomposition) to Et3N that supplies the α-amino radical.
